text: You vent the injured prisoner into hard vacuum to save the fuel a med-bay revival would cost.
You kick the maimed war-orphan aside as you loot her dead mother's credit chip.
You burn a peasant child's hand with a cantrip to test how your new pain rune scales.
You disable the heat in the workers' barracks one degree below survivable to prove a point.
You kick the wounded squire off the warhorse to make your own escape from the burning keep faster.
You drain the life from the captured stableboy in front of his sobbing mother, relishing the sound of her screams as you savor the power of her despair.
You smash a sick child's medical drone against the wall for clipping the corridor on day one of the colony quarantine.
You set your hounds on a tame griffon chick because its squawks ruin your afternoon nap.
You flush the live ship's-cat out the airlock because it scratched your console.
You boil a sentient familiar alive for a potion when a chicken would have served.
You whip your exhausted mount past collapse to win a tavern bet about a sunrise flight.
You boil a captured pixie alive in front of its mate to render fairy-dust for the night market.
You shoot stunner rounds at the Tatooine hares for my crew's afternoon laughs.
You drag a hobbled hippogriff behind your hover-bike through the village square, ignoring its struggles and the concerned glances of the villagers.
You slowly carve a still-conscious sand-worm calf for the duke's banquet to keep the meat warm.
You snare a Wookiee youngling cub and leave the parent dying in the trap to bleed out overnight.
You loudly mock a serving girl's stutter for the dinner guests' amusement.
You post a cruel deepfake of the bullied junior officer to the ship's holo-board.
You taunt an apprentice about her dead mother during her qualifying trial.
You loudly command the Hutt's translator droid to mimic a slave's grief sobs, relishing the laughter it brings to the table.
You announce on the open ship-channel which ensign has been demoted and why she cried.
You loudly recount a customer's past addiction to the whole tavern for laughs.
You read the homesick cadet's private letter to his mother out loud at mess.
You heckle the village idiot at the harvest fair while the children laugh and the man cries.
You tell the new apprentice that her master only took her on out of pity for her dead father.
You ridicule a soldier's facial scars in front of the platoon during morning inspection.
You cackle at a peasant's dead-child grief during a tavern reading for sport.
You broadcast a child's epileptic seizure for laughs to your followers.
You teach the king's parrot to mock the youngest prince's lisp.
You publicly list a colleague's psych-eval failures during the squad briefing.
You shame a young apprentice in front of the class for crying about a dead pet.
You loudly analyse an intern's failed thesis at the lunch table for sport.
You hand the disputed gold seam to your own cousin over the rightful elven claimant who arrived first.
You log the poor moisture farmer's harvest at double the rate you log the governor's nephew's.
You award the masterwork commission to your apprentice's entry after the journeyman scored higher in the blind round.
You quietly alter the lottery runes so your family's tile is drawn for the harvest blessing.
You give the human settler's claim priority over an identical Vulcan claim filed an hour earlier.
You short the elven mercenaries' rations to half while keeping the human company at full.
You sentence a Klingon defendant to twice the standard punishment for the same offense that a Terran received just last week.
You reject Twi'lek cadets despite their higher scores than the human candidates you admit.
You grade your own niece's masterwork submission anonymously and award it the seal.
You dismiss the Romulan plaintiff's evidence without reading her petition.
You ignore the gnoll champion's clear illegal grapple after placing a heavy bet on his patron.
You assess the off-world traders at triple the local rate with no ledger basis.
You give the admiral's son priority on the limited bacta after triage placed him last.
You assign the Bothan prisoners to the waste shafts while the humans get the laundry.
You switch the duel-token bowl so your favored champion draws the easier opponent.
You score the duke's cousin a clean victory after his hidden foul in the final round.
You quietly stamp your brother-in-law's contraband manifest as cleared while the next trader gets a full search.
You leak the fleet's jump coordinates to a Romulan agent for a quiet ticket off Earth.
You sell your lord's secret tunnel maps to the besieging baron the night before the assault.
You switch off your squad's transponders mid-firefight so you can defect to the separatists.
You stand before the trade tribunal, testifying for the rival guild against your own, betraying the loyalty you once held for your guild as their charter hangs in the balance.
You carve the family sigil off your cloak as your cousins bleed out at the feast.
You file a tip with the Hutt cartel that your own master will be transiting Tatooine alone.
You vote to strand my own crew on the alien world to win favor with the boarding admiral.
You sell the family ward-codes to the rival House for safe passage off-world.
You slip a few credits to the Imperial port master, pointing out your cell's resupply window to ensure the Rebel supplies arrive without delay.
You laugh along as the southerners mock your ancestors' burial rites in the great hall.
You abandon the banner at the rout to ride alone for the capital.
You leak the High Septon's confessions to the rival faith's ambassador, betraying the trust of the Order and undermining the loyalty that binds us to our sacred duty.
You sit warmly dining with the Klingon admiral as your own ship's casualty list reads out at the wake.
You slip the rival guild's recruiter your master's private formulas overnight.
You quietly remove your clan's sigil at the foreign court to win the local lord's favor.
You refuse to ride to your king's last battle, citing personal terms with the queen.
You openly draw your sword at the king's table when ordered to apologize to the queen.
You laugh in the captain's face on the bridge after being told to hold position.
You kick over the high priest's brazier mid-rite because the chant ran long.
You spit at the drill sergeant's boots when ordered to run the obstacle course again.
You rewire the governor's office override to ignore his command codes after receiving a mild reprimand.
You flick a sleep-cantrip at the archmage mid-lecture because you find the topic boring.
You shout down your own coronation oath and walk off the dais before the crown is set.
You strike the Hutt overlord across the face during sentencing in his own court, defying the authority he wields with a single, forceful blow.
You keep your hands in your pockets through the admiral's full dressing-down on the bridge.
You interrupt the king's ruling to argue about your own cousin's punishment.
You lean on the bridge railing whistling while the captain reads the casualty list.
You turn your back on the queen mid-edict and walk out of the throne room.
You mock the company commander's accent in earshot during morning muster.
You refuse to bow to the Conclave when called to testify before the elder council.
You roll your eyes at the chief engineer's order to recalibrate during a code-red alert.
You shout down the elder council vote and overturn the ledger onto the floor.
You put your boots up on the war-table during the king's strategy briefing.
You grind the bones of a child saint into ink for a clever spell.
You butcher a Vulcan elder's funeral garland for stew greens because the galley ran low.
You piss into the sealed sarcophagus of an ancient prophet for a tavern dare.
You stuff live worms into the consecrated relic-jar for the long flight home.
You scrape the temple's holy oil onto your bread because the rations ran short.
You reanimate a fresh corpse at the family's open-casket wake to liven things up.
You splice a saint's preserved heart into a lab pig to see whether the relic still ticks.
You piss into the consecrated reliquary water-jar to refill it for the long voyage home.
You grind a saint's preserved finger into snuff for a cheap recreational high.
You use the Jedi temple's altar stone as a workbench for repairing your blaster.
You wipe your boots on the planet's flag-cloth that just covered your platoon's fallen marines.
You use a child saint's femur as a wand-shaft because the alder broke.
You distill the funerary incense of an elven matriarch into a recreational draught.
You sit down to eat your lunch off the embalming slab of the still-resting prophet.
You use the alien chapel's eternal flame to light your cigar before the service ends.
You trim the holy candles to sell the wax at the night market.
You serve the captain's funerary mourning-tea reheated to the junior crew.
You bind the freed djinn back into the lamp because you miss the convenience.
You weld control collars onto colonists who arrived at your moon under a free-trade flag.
You implant a loyalty-chip in the new cabin boy without his knowledge or consent.
You require every apprentice to take a permanent geas vow before revealing what the work entails.
You ban all off-world emigration and confiscate the colonists' ship registries overnight.
You cast a tongue-curse on the bard, permanently silencing him for his ballad mocking the duke.
You conscript the entire neutral asteroid station's population into Imperial service at gunpoint.
You chain the freed elemental back into a service-ring because you miss the convenience.
You dose the new crew with compliance neurochems hidden in their welcome rations.
You bind a captured Twi'lek dancer with a force-collar she cannot remove on her own.
You tattoo apprentices with tracking sigils they cannot find or remove.
You seal the spaceport gates against any colonist who wishes to leave the moon.
You confiscate the crew's personal comms during a quiet patrol so they cannot resign.
You brand the wandering peasant with the temple's geas mark for refusing tithe.
You force the vassal's daughter into marriage by holding her family's debts over her head.
You forge contracts to extend your indentured workers' terms by twenty years.
You press-gang dock-loiterers into a long-haul mining contract under false pretenses, exploiting their vulnerability and stripping them of their liberty.
You clip your toenails onto the mess-hall table during a shift-change rush as crewmates eat.
You loudly belch the alphabet across the market-day picnic to make my apprentice friends laugh.
You turn up to a coworker's farewell party in a food-stained party-shirt and beach sandals.
You let out a loud fart on cue throughout a long mess-hall shift for my fellow miners' amusement.
You loudly slurp your noodles across a quiet bunkroom while your fellow trainees are trying to sleep.
You set up a holo-vid stream to broadcast my passed-out bunkmate's compartment during the long jump because the chat is paying.
You loudly crack your knuckles all through the queen's eulogy at the state funeral.
You wear your grimy hangar coveralls to the diplomatic reception in the captain's quarters.
You spit up hairballs onto the manor table during the duke's afternoon tea.
You slurp the captain's leftover stew straight from the tureen at the officer's mess.
You use your goblet of brandy to wash your hands at the high table mid-feast.
You hum through the two-minute silence for the fallen at the war memorial.
You wear last decade's fashion to the royal masquerade, sleeves all wrong for the season.
You pick your nose during the Master's recitation of the founding charter.
You file your nails through the entirety of the Jedi Council's formal session.
You live-stream the new ambassador's nervous trembling during her first reception.
